title: Mental Availability
question: Is the brand easy to think of in buying situations?
definition: The brand's ability to come to mind for category buyers across useful category entry points.
hbgRole: Core How Brands Grow growth lever.
guardrail: Do not reduce this to generic awareness. Mental availability is about useful retrieval paths in buying situations.

title: Distinctive Memory Structures
question: Is the brand easy to recognize as itself?
definition: The brand's recognizable assets, codes, packaging, and creative cues that help people notice and retrieve the brand.
hbgRole: Makes mental availability easier to build and refresh.
guardrail: BBE Different is not the same as distinctive asset strength. Treat Different as adjacent equity evidence only.

title: Physical Availability
question: Is the brand easy to find and buy?
definition: The brand's presence, prominence, portfolio availability, and buyability across channels and retailers.
hbgRole: Core How Brands Grow growth lever alongside mental availability.
guardrail: Do not infer physical availability from equity scores. It requires commercial, retail, or ecommerce evidence.

title: Momentum
question: Is the brand getting healthier, or merely large today?
definition: The directional read of whether equity, mental availability, commercial conversion, and follow-up proof are compounding.
hbgRole: Prevents the system from confusing current size with future health.
guardrail: Do not call one period a durable trend. Momentum needs repeated evidence or a clear recency caveat.

title: Machine Availability
question: Is the brand easy for AI, search, retail algorithms, and answer engines to understand and recommend?
definition: The brand's discoverability, retrievability, and actionability through AI-mediated and algorithmic shopping environments.
hbgRole: A 2026 applied extension of availability, not an official HBG law.
guardrail: Label this as an AI-era availability read. Do not present it as an official Ehrenberg-Bass construct.

title: Portfolio Growth Availability
question: Does the portfolio catch buyers as needs, occasions, and life contexts evolve?
definition: The portfolio-level view of brand roles, CEP coverage, whitespace, overlap, and growth pools.
hbgRole: Applies availability thinking across brands rather than treating each brand as isolated.
guardrail: Portfolio overlap is not cannibalization proof. Treat it as a strategic question, not a measured migration fact.
